bands.raw_text: Students receive a bachelor's degree in the Humanities or Commerce faculty (BA/BSocSci or BCom) at the end of their first three years of study, and a bachelor's degree in law (LLB) at the end of a further two years. Students must apply for admission to the Law Faculty at the end of their undergraduate Humanities or Commerce degree and must obtain a cumulative GPA of at least 60% in their UCT undergraduate degree in order to proceed to the two-year graduate LLB degree.

FACULTY OF LAW.req_text: Students receive a bachelor's degree in the Humanities or Commerce faculty (BA/BSocSci or BCom) at the end of their first three years of study, and a bachelor's degree in law (LLB) at the end of a further two years. Students must apply for admission to the Law Faculty at the end of their undergraduate Humanities or Commerce degree and must obtain a cumulative GPA of at least 60% in their UCT undergraduate degree in order to proceed to the two-year graduate LLB degree.

bands.raw_text: Requires at least 6 years of study. Students must apply for admission to the Law Faculty at the end of their undergraduate degree and must obtain a cumulative GPA of at least 60% in their UCT undergraduate degree in order to proceed to the graduate LLB degree.

FACULTY OF LAW.req_text: Requires at least 6 years of study. Students must apply for admission to the Law Faculty at the end of their undergraduate degree and must obtain a cumulative GPA of at least 60% in their UCT undergraduate degree in order to proceed to the graduate LLB degree.
